_doc: Reference templates for editing config.json in the cs2388/STRCconfig repo. This file is NOT loaded by the STRCTracker app. It exists only as documentation for manual edits. Keys prefixed with '_' (like '_when_to_use', '_notes') are comments. Real schema fields have no underscore prefix.
_workflow: 1. Identify a filing where the parser produced wrong numbers.
2. Pick the appropriate template below (strc / mstr / mixed).
4. Paste into config.json's confirmedPurchases array, prepending so it's most-recent-first.
5. Edit placeholder values (date, btc, totalValueUSD, etc.) with the real corrected numbers.
6. Commit + push config.json to GitHub. CDN propagates in ~5min.
7. App's next config refresh applies the override.
_app_behavior: Per-date precedence: config entries REPLACE any auto-parsed entry for the same date (patch292). EDGAR continues to populate dates not in config (patch293 gap-fill). To revert a correction, delete the entry from config.json and push — EDGAR resumes for that date.

strc_funded_correction._when_to_use: Parser extracted wrong STRC slice from an 8-K where STRC was a real funding source. The week may have been pure-STRC or mixed (STRC + MSTR). Replace 'btc' with the STRC-funded BTC slice and 'totalValueUSD' with STRC-funded proceeds USD. Set 'totalWeeklyBTC' / 'totalWeeklyProceeds' to the strategy-wide totals (the BTC Update table in the 8-K).
strc_funded_correction._headline_field: The BIG BTC NUMBER on Dashboard + Activity cards comes from 'totalWeeklyBTC' (the strategy-wide total). Set this to the full weekly purchase from the 8-K's BTC Update table. The 'btc' field below is only the STRC-attributable slice — shown as 'STRC FUNDED · $X · NN%' attribution line, NOT the headline. If you only fill in 'btc' and leave 'totalWeeklyBTC' out, the headline falls back to 'btc' (smaller number) and the card loses its attribution lines.
_notes: Required: date, btc, totalValueUSD, avgPriceUSD, filingDescription, secURL.
Without weekly totals, card falls through to simple 'CAPTURED $X' layout.
secURL: copy from EDGAR — must be parseable as URL.

entry.filingDescription: 8-K MMM D, YYYY · STRC period MMM D-MMM D

mstr_only_correction._when_to_use: Parser misread an MSTR-only week as STRC-funded, OR Strategy filed an MSTR-only week and parser failed to produce any entry at all. Set 'btc' / 'totalValueUSD' to 0 (no STRC slice). Set 'totalWeeklyBTC' / 'totalWeeklyProceeds' to the full weekly purchase. fundingSource MUST be 'mstr' or the YTD chart will incorrectly include this entry.
mstr_only_correction._headline_field: The BIG BTC NUMBER on Dashboard + Activity cards comes from 'totalWeeklyBTC'. For MSTR-only weeks this IS the full weekly purchase (no STRC slice exists to compare against). The 'btc' field MUST be 0 because no STRC-attributable BTC was acquired this week. Card renders as: '<totalWeeklyBTC> BTC' headline + 'MSTR FUNDED · $<totalWeeklyProceeds> · 100%' attribution.
_notes: btc and totalValueUSD = 0 because no STRC funded this week.
totalWeeklyBTC and totalWeeklyProceeds = full purchase numbers.
fundingSource = 'mstr' is REQUIRED — otherwise YTD chart inflates.
Activity card will render: '3,273 BTC' headline + 'MSTR FUNDED · $X · 100%'.

mixed_funding_correction._when_to_use: Future use: filing where multiple preferred classes (STRC + STRK + STRF, etc.) jointly funded a purchase batch. Currently rare — Strategy mostly mixes STRC and MSTR ATMs. Set 'btc' / 'totalValueUSD' to the STRC-attributable slice. The 'mixed' fundingSource currently routes to STRC-funded display logic; may need parser/view updates if STRK / STRF start funding.
mixed_funding_correction._headline_field: The BIG BTC NUMBER on Dashboard + Activity cards comes from 'totalWeeklyBTC' (full strategy-wide purchase across all funding sources). The 'btc' field is the STRC-attributable slice only. View renders 'MSTR FUNDED' as headline minus STRC slice, which on truly-mixed weeks (STRC+STRK+MSTR) will lump non-STRC slices together under 'MSTR FUNDED' label — slightly wrong but cosmetic. Fix at parser level when STRK/STRF/STRD start funding.
_notes: Until parser handles per-security extraction, the View shows MSTR-derived as (total - STRC) which will be slightly wrong on truly-mixed (STRC + STRK + MSTR) weeks.

_top_level_overrides._doc: These are not in confirmedPurchases — they are top-level keys in config.json itself. Listed here for reference.

strategyTotalBTC._when_to_use: Update Strategy's total BTC holdings displayed on Dashboard. Should match the 'As of' figure from the most recent 8-K. Note: app uses MONOTONIC guard — only larger values win. Cannot decrease via config (Strategy doesn't sell BTC, so this is fine in practice).

strcYield._when_to_use: Update current STRC monthly yield rate (annualized). Strategy adjusts this monthly. Match to most recent yield-change 8-K. Range 0-50, typically 9-12.

_daily_overrides._doc: RemoteOverride entries go in the top-level 'overrides' array (not 'confirmedPurchases'). Used to correct daily ATM estimate cards (not 8-K filings). Each entry replaces the snapshot/seed for that single day.
_daily_overrides._headline_field: Unlike confirmedPurchases entries, the 'btc' field here IS the big BTC number on the daily estimate card. There is no separate 'totalWeekly...' field for daily overrides because each entry represents a single day's estimated activity, not a weekly total.

entry._btc_note: Optional. Drives the headline BTC number on the daily card. Omit for zero-activity days.
entry._proceeds_note: Optional. USD value shown alongside BTC.
entry.reason: self-documenting note for why this override exists
